edges.description: Circulating AQP4-IgG requires CNS access through barrier-vulnerable sites or BBB disruption.
AQP4-IgG production provides the antibody substrate for astrocyte binding.
Barrier leak enables circulating anti-AQP4 antibodies to engage astrocytic targets.
Antibody-coated astrocytes become substrates for complement-mediated injury.
Astrocyte dysfunction disrupts homeostatic support for oligodendrocytes and neurons.
Complement effector activity amplifies downstream cytokine and immune-cell responses.
Complement-mediated astrocyte loss propagates tissue-destructive downstream injury.
Th17-skewed cytokine signaling promotes further innate immune recruitment and lesion propagation.
Inflammatory astrocytopathy and demyelination frequently affect the optic nerves.
Granulocyte-rich inflammatory lesions in the spinal cord produce LETM.
Barrier-vulnerable brainstem regions such as the area postrema are frequent symptomatic targets.
Area postrema and dorsal medulla involvement in AQP4-positive NMOSD produces intractable hiccups as one component of area postrema syndrome.
Secondary demyelinating injury in the optic pathways drives visual attacks.
Destructive demyelination in the spinal cord produces LETM and severe myelopathic disability.

nodes.description: Peripheral B-lineage cells generate pathogenic AQP4-IgG, creating the circulating autoantibody substrate for astrocyte-targeted NMOSD attacks.

evidence.reference_title: Inebilizumab for the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (N-MOmentum): a double-blind, randomised placebo-controlled phase 2/3 trial.
evidence.snippet: of inebilizumab, an anti-CD19, B cell-depleting antibody, in reducing the risk of attacks and disability in NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: Satralizumab: A Review in Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: which is designed to suppress autoantibody production by blocking the interleukin-6 (IL-6) receptor

nodes.description: Pathogenic NMO-IgG binds extracellular AQP4 on perivascular astrocytic endfeet, with higher-order orthogonal arrays of particles increasing antibody avidity and pathogenicity.

evidence.reference_title: Aquaporin-4 in Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorders: A Target of Autoimmunity in the Central Nervous System.
evidence.snippet: In NMOSD, the autoantibody (NMO-IgG) binds to the extracellular loops of AQP4 as expressed in perivascular astrocytic end-feet and disrupts astrocytes in a complement-dependent manner.

nodes.description: Intractable nausea, vomiting, and hiccups caused by lesions in the area postrema, a barrier-vulnerable circumventricular region that is highly characteristic of AQP4-positive NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: International consensus diagnostic criteria for neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: The core clinical characteristics required for patients with NMOSD with AQP4-IgG include clinical syndromes or MRI findings related to optic nerve, spinal cord, area postrema

nodes.description: Early blood-brain barrier dysfunction permits circulating AQP4 autoantibodies to enter the central nervous system and reach vulnerable perivascular astrocyte endfeet.

evidence.reference_title: Blood–Brain Barrier Disruption in Neuroimmunological Disease
evidence.snippet: At the onset of NMOSD, increased permeability of the BBB causes the entry of circulating AQP4 autoantibodies into the central nervous system (CNS).

nodes.description: Anti-AQP4 immune complexes activate the classical complement pathway, generating terminal complement-mediated astrocyte injury and driving the complement-sensitive relapse biology of AQP4-positive disease.

evidence.reference_title: Eculizumab in Aquaporin-4-Positive Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: At least two thirds of cases are associated with aquaporin-4 antibodies (AQP4-IgG) and complement-mediated damage to the central nervous system.

nodes.description: AQP4 immune complexes stimulate complement-dependent IL-6 and IL-17A release, reinforcing a Th17-skewed inflammatory program and sustaining relapse-prone neuroinflammation.

evidence.reference_title: Anti-aquaporin-4 immune complex stimulates complement-dependent Th17 cytokine release in neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders
evidence.snippet: Co-stimulation of PBMCs with AQP4-IgG/AQP4 immunocomplex and complement prompts a Th17-biased response consistent with the inflammatory paradigm observed in NMOSD.

nodes.description: Anti-C5 complement blockade directly targets the complement-dependent astrocytopathic mechanism of anti-AQP4 NMOSD and reduces relapse risk.

evidence.reference_title: Eculizumab in Aquaporin-4-Positive Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Adjudicated relapses occurred in 3 of 96 patients (3%) in the eculizumab group and 20 of 47 (43%) in the placebo group

nodes.description: AQP4-IgG-based models reproduce granulocyte and macrophage infiltration, capturing the neutrophil- and eosinophil-rich inflammatory pattern that distinguishes AQP4-positive NMOSD lesions from multiple sclerosis.

evidence.reference_title: Aquaporin-4-IgG-seropositive neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders: progress of experimental models based on disease pathogenesis
evidence.snippet: These experimental models have successfully simulated many pathological features of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders, such as aquaporin-4 loss, astrocytopathy, granulocyte and macrophage infiltration, complement activation, demyelination, and neuronal loss;

nodes.description: Anti-CD19 B-lineage depletion reduces the cellular source of pathogenic AQP4 autoantibody production and lowers attack risk.

evidence.reference_title: Inebilizumab for the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (N-MOmentum): a double-blind, randomised placebo-controlled phase 2/3 trial.
evidence.snippet: 21 (12%) of 174 participants receiving inebilizumab had an attack versus 22 (39%) of 56 participants receiving placebo

nodes.description: Intractable hiccups are a frequent component of area postrema syndrome in AQP4-IgG-positive NMOSD.

evidence.reference_title: Area postrema syndrome: Frequency, criteria, and severity in AQP4-IgG-positive NMOSD.
evidence.snippet: and hiccups (102, 65%) lasted a median of 14 days (2-365).

nodes.description: Long spinal cord lesions spanning three or more vertebral segments are a hallmark attack pattern in anti-AQP4 NMOSD and often drive severe motor, sensory, and sphincter deficits.

evidence.reference_title: International consensus diagnostic criteria for neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: The core clinical characteristics required for patients with NMOSD with AQP4-IgG include clinical syndromes or MRI findings related to optic nerve, spinal cord, area postrema

nodes.description: Acute, often severe optic nerve inflammation causing visual loss and pain, frequently with bilateral or highly disabling attacks in AQP4-positive disease.

evidence.reference_title: International consensus diagnostic criteria for neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders.
evidence.snippet: The core clinical characteristics required for patients with NMOSD with AQP4-IgG include clinical syndromes or MRI findings related to optic nerve, spinal cord, area postrema, other brainstem, diencephalic, or cerebral presentations.

nodes.description: IL-6 receptor blockade interferes with the cytokine-amplification loop that supports plasmablast survival and Th17-skewed inflammation in anti-AQP4 disease.

evidence.reference_title: Satralizumab: A Review in Neuromyelitis Optica Spectrum Disorder.
evidence.snippet: Satralizumab (Enspryng®) is a monoclonal antibody that blocks the interleukin-6 (IL-6) receptor and is approved for the treatment of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (NMOSD) in patients who are aquaporin-4 immunoglobulin G (AQP4-IgG) seropositive.

nodes.description: Astrocyte-targeted injury leads secondarily to demyelination and neuronal loss rather than primary oligodendrocyte autoimmunity, explaining the destructive opticospinal phenotype of anti-AQP4 disease.

evidence.reference_title: Aquaporin-4-IgG-seropositive neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders: progress of experimental models based on disease pathogenesis
evidence.snippet: These experimental models have successfully simulated many pathological features of neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorders, such as aquaporin-4 loss, astrocytopathy, granulocyte and macrophage infiltration, complement activation, demyelination, and neuronal loss;
